originalTitle: Sunrise Movement takes credit for disrupting Trump’s New York state rally – US politics live
summary: Activists arrested at rally in Rockland county belong to youth-led climate justice group, spokesperson says
content: According to a marriage certificate obtained by TMZ, a celebrity gossip site, the president’s oldest son, Donald Trump Jr, was officially married for the second time, to socialite Bettina Anderson, on Thursday in West Palm Beach, Florida. The president said earlier this week that he was too busy with the war he started, but has not been able to end, with Iran to attend the celebration of the couple’s marriage planned for the Bahamas on Saturday. Don Jr was previously married for 13 years to Vanessa Trump, until the couple divorced in 2018 after having have five children together. From 2020 to 2024, he was engaged to Kimberly Guilfoyle, a former Fox News host who was previously married to Gavin Newsom, when the Democrat was mayor of San Francisco. Guilfoyle gave a memorable speech at the pandemic-era 2020 Republican National Convention, when she shouted most of her remarks to the nearly empty Andrew W. Mellon Auditorium in Washiongton, and concluded with the promise: “the best is yet to come!” In December 2024, then president-elect Donald Trump announced that he was nominating Guilfoyle to be US ambassador to Greece just hours after the Daily Mail published the first photographs of Don Jr holding hands with Anderson in Palm Beach. The Sunrise Movement, the youth-led climate justice organization that popularized calls for a Green New Deal, took credit for disrupting Donald Trump’s rally in New York state on Friday. “The activists arrested were activists with Sunrise Movement,” a spokesperson for the group confirmed to the Guardian in an email. Video posted on social media by the movement, recorded at two points during Trump’s speech to supporters at Rockland Community College, showed two activists protesting the president’s mass deportation campaign and his war on Iran. The first activist stood on a chair and shouted: “Donald Trump you’re separating families! Immigrants are under attack, how do you sleep at night?” After the activist asked, “how many people are in ICE camps?” and shouted “ICE killed-” at the president, a police officer appeared and yanked him off the seat. “Go home to mom,” Trump said with derision. “Go home.” Minutes later, on the opposite side of the hall, a second activist held up a sign that read: “Trump is a war criminal” and shouted “You should be ashamed of yourself!” “You’re a war criminal,” she continued over loud boos from the Trump supporters around her. “How dare you take our money and send it to Iran to kill children! How dare you send it to Gaza to engage in a genocide!” As the world waits to see if he will order military strike on Iran, or Cuba, Donald Trump is spending his Friday night at the White House posting AI generated images of himself, tossing Stephen Colbert into a dumpster in one, and menacing Greenland in another. A US judge dismissed a criminal indictment against Kilmar Ábrego García on Friday, finding that the Trump administration would not have prosecuted him had he not challenged his high-profile deportation. Ábrego, who had entered the United States without authorization in the past, became a symbol of the Trump administration’s drive for mass deportations when he was sent to the notorious anti-terrorism mega-prison in El Salvador known as Cecot in March 2025. “Thank you to God, my attorneys, We Are Casa, and everyone who has continued to support the fight for justice,” said Ábrego in a statement provided by We Are Casa, an immigrant rights organization in Maryland, where he lives, that assisted in his legal representation. He added: “Justice is a big word and an even bigger promise to fulfill; and I am grateful that today, justice has taken a step forward.” Along with 260 other people, chiefly Venezuelans, the administration sent Ábrego to the Salvadorian prison on 15 March last year, despite a US federal judge’s order to stop the flights and grave accusations of human rights abuses there. The Trump administration sent him to El Salvador despite a prior US court order barring him from being returned there because of a risk of persecution. After sending Ábrego to the anti-terrorism prison, the Trump administration admitted they had mistakenly done so, blaming an “administrative error”. Amid speculation about possible US military strikes on Iran, or Cuba, over the weekend, Donald Trump just flew back to the White House after his rally in Rockland county, New York. That marked a change of plan from the schedule released by the White House on Thursday, which said that Trump would spend the weekend at his New Jersey golf club in Bedminster, which is just an hour’s drive from the site of his rally. Trump’s return to the White House led some observers to suggest that military operations might be the real reason he is not going to his son Don Jr’s wedding celebration on a private island in the Bahamas on Saturday. “While I very much wanted to be with my son, Don Jr., and the newest member of the Trump Family, his soon to be wife, Bettina, circumstances pertaining to Government, and my love for the United States of America, do not allow me to do so,” Trump wrote on social media earlier on Friday. “I feel it is important for me to remain in Washington, D.C., at the White House during this important period of time.” “Trump invading Cuba this weekend to get out of going to his son’s wedding would be very on brand”, the Pod Save America host Dan Pfeiffer joked in response to senator Lindsey Graham’s post that “the liberation” of Cuba “is close at hand”. Senator Lindsey Graham, once a fierce opponent of Donald Trump turned enthusiastic supporter, suggested on social media on Friday that a US military attack on Cuba to topple its government might be imminent. “I believe the liberation of the wonderful people of Cuba from the clutches of communism is close at hand,” Graham wrote. “Cuba Libre.” Donald Trump just completed what was effectively a campaign rally for Republicans in New York state, leaving the stage to the strains of the song he called “the gay
chineseTitle: 日出运动因扰乱特朗普纽约州集会而受到赞誉——美国政治直播
chineseSummary: 发言人称，在罗克兰县集会上被捕的活动人士属于青年领导的气候正义组织
chineseContent: 根据名人八卦网站 TMZ 获得的结婚证，总统的长子小唐纳德·特朗普周四在佛罗里达州西棕榈滩与社交名流贝蒂娜·安德森第二次正式结婚。总统本周早些时候表示，他太忙于他发起的战争，但一直未能结束与伊朗的战争，无法参加计划于周六在巴哈马举行的这对夫妇的结婚庆典。小唐此前与凡妮莎·特朗普结婚 13 年，直到两人在育有 5 个孩子后于 2018 年离婚。 2020年至2024年，他与前福克斯新闻主持人金伯利·吉尔福伊尔(Kimberly Guilfoyle)订婚，金伯利·吉尔福伊尔此前曾与民主党人担任旧金山市长的加文·纽瑟姆(Gavin Newsom)结婚。吉尔福伊尔在疫情期间的 2020 年共和党全国代表大会上发表了令人难忘的演讲，当时她对着华盛顿几乎空无一人的安德鲁·W·梅隆礼堂大声喊出了自己的大部分讲话，并以承诺结束：“最好的尚未到来！” 2024 年 12 月，时任总统当选人唐纳德·特朗普宣布提名吉尔福伊尔担任美国驻希腊大使，就在《每日邮报》刊登小唐与安德森在棕榈滩牵手的第一张照片几小时后。日出运动是一个由青年领导的气候正义组织，广泛呼吁绿色新政，该组织因扰乱唐纳德·特朗普周五在纽约州的集会而受到赞誉。 “被捕的活动人士是日出运动的活动人士，”该组织的发言人在一封电子邮件中向《卫报》证实。该运动在社交媒体上发布的视频是特朗普在罗克兰社区学院向支持者发表演讲期间录制的两个时间点，视频显示两名活动人士抗议总统的大规模驱逐运动和对伊朗的战争。时间
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
